required_steps.reach_out_to_residence_progress_notes:
On 2025-09-10, youth (Jordan) and coach identified three supportive housing residences that accept young adults with an APPLA goal: (1) Harbor House Transitional Living, (2) Maple Grove Supportive Residence, and (3) Eastside Bridge Housing Program. On 2025-09-12, Jordan called Harbor House using the intake number listed on their website and spoke briefly with the intake coordinator, Ms. Alvarez, who explained their eligibility requirements (ages 18–24, in or recently aged out of foster care, some income, and willingness to participate in life-skills programming). Jordan wrote down basic information including expected length of stay (up to 24 months) and curfew rules. 

On 2025-09-13, coach helped Jordan draft a short email introduction that could be re-used for other residences. Jordan then emailed Maple Grove Supportive Residence to request an application packet and clarification on waitlist times. Maple Grove responded on 2025-09-14, confirming that they are accepting applications and that their average wait time is 2–3 months. 

On 2025-09-15, Jordan completed a follow-up call with Eastside Bridge Housing Program during a scheduled coaching session, using speakerphone so the coach could support if needed. Jordan asked about expectations around employment, substance use policy, and support for continuing college. Eastside Bridge confirmed that they encourage enrollment in school or employment and offer on-site case management. 

Overall, Jordan has successfully reached out to three residences, gathered information on eligibility and expectations, and obtained application instructions for Harbor House and Maple Grove. Jordan reported feeling nervous making the phone calls but said it got easier after the first one. Coach provided encouragement and helped Jordan prepare a short script with key questions (e.g., "What documents do you need from me?", "How long is the waitlist right now?").
required_steps.complete_application_progress_notes:
Jordan has started, but not yet fully completed, applications for two supportive housing residences. On 2025-09-20, Jordan and coach downloaded and printed the Harbor House Transitional Living application. Jordan filled out the sections about personal information, foster care history, current school enrollment at Central Community College, and part-time employment at FreshMart Grocery. Jordan left blank the sections requesting detailed mental health history and emergency contacts, planning to complete those later after confirming information with their therapist and caseworker. 

On 2025-09-27, Jordan began the Maple Grove Supportive Residence online application using a shared computer at the youth resource center. Jordan completed demographic information and answered narrative questions about why they are seeking supportive housing. Jordan wrote about wanting a stable, quiet place to focus on finishing their associate degree in business administration, reduce stress related to couch-surfing, and work more consistent hours at their job. Jordan also wrote about how living in multiple foster homes has made it hard to feel secure, and how having a stable place could help them maintain routines like cooking, cleaning, and studying. 

As of the latest check-in on 2025-10-05, both applications are partially complete. Harbor House application is about 70% done; missing pieces include updated pay stubs, verification of enrollment from the college, and a short personal statement about housing goals. Maple Grove application is about 60% done; Jordan still needs to upload copies of their state ID and Social Security card and answer a few more questions about past housing situations and conflict resolution. Jordan and coach have scheduled time on 2025-10-12 to finish both applications and to scan and upload required documents at the resource center. Because the applications are not yet submitted, this step is currently marked as not completed (NO), even though substantial progress has been made.

potential_steps.submit_any_other_required_documentation_:
Jordan has identified and gathered most of the documentation required by Harbor House and Maple Grove but has not yet submitted full packets to either program. Required documents include: copy of state ID, Social Security card, most recent pay stubs (last 30 days), proof of enrollment at Central Community College, a brief letter from Jordan's current caseworker confirming foster care status and APPLA goal, and, for Harbor House, a short letter from a mental health provider describing any current treatment or accommodations. 

Between 2025-09-25 and 2025-10-05, Jordan located their original Social Security card and birth certificate in a file folder kept in their current foster home. On 2025-09-29, coach accompanied Jordan to the DMV to request a replacement state ID because the previous one had expired. Jordan received the new state ID in the mail on 2025-10-04. Jordan also asked their supervisor at FreshMart to reprint pay stubs for the last four weeks; these were picked up on 2025-10-06. 

Jordan emailed their academic advisor at Central Community College on 2025-10-01 to request a verification-of-enrollment letter, which was provided as a PDF on 2025-10-07. Jordan then reached out to their therapist, Dr. Kelsey Raymond, on 2025-10-03 to ask for a short letter confirming ongoing therapy and any housing-related recommendations (such as preference for a quieter environment and access to on-site support staff). Dr. Raymond agreed and is preparing a brief letter, expected by mid-October. 

As of 2025-10-08, all documents except the therapist letter have been gathered but not yet scanned or uploaded. Jordan and coach plan to use the resource center scanner on 2025-10-12 to upload these documents with the applications. Because the full documentation packets have not yet been submitted to Harbor House or Maple Grove, this step is still marked as not completed (NO), even though Jordan has made significant progress in gathering everything needed.
potential_steps.other: Attend at least one in-person or virtual information session or tour for a supportive housing residence to better understand rules, expectations, and daily life.
potential_steps.other_progress_notes:
Jordan attended a virtual information session for Harbor House Transitional Living on 2025-09-30 from 5:30 PM to 6:30 PM via Zoom. The session was led by the program director and a peer mentor currently living at Harbor House. During the session, staff explained the program's core expectations, including weekly house meetings, curfew at 11:00 PM on weeknights (midnight on weekends), and participation in at least one life-skills workshop per month (topics such as budgeting, cooking, conflict resolution, and job readiness). They also emphasized that residents are expected to keep their rooms clean, complete assigned chores in shared spaces, and communicate in advance if they are going to be late or miss a meeting. 

Jordan asked several questions during the Q&A portion, including whether overnight guests are allowed (answer: occasional family visits with prior approval, but no overnight romantic partners), how they handle conflicts between roommates, and what kind of support is available for residents who are attending college while working part-time. Jordan appreciated hearing from the peer mentor, who shared that they also came from a foster care background and initially found it hard to adjust to the rules but eventually appreciated the structure. 

After the session, Jordan shared with the coach that they felt more confident about applying to Harbor House because they liked the idea of on-site staff and clear expectations, although they were a little worried about curfew since they sometimes work late evening shifts at FreshMart. Coach and Jordan discussed strategies for requesting schedule adjustments at work or asking Harbor House staff if exceptions can be made for work-related late arrivals. Because Jordan attended the full session, took notes, and followed up with the coach to process the information and questions, this step is marked as completed (YES).

goal_tracking.goal_completed_no_notes:
As of the most recent review on 2025-10-10, Jordan has not yet fully completed the overall goal of successfully applying to other supportive housing residences and having at least one application formally submitted and under review. While Jordan has made meaningful progress—researching appropriate programs, reaching out to multiple residences, starting two applications (Harbor House and Maple Grove), attending an information session, and gathering most of the required documentation—the applications themselves are still in draft form and have not been officially submitted. 

Key remaining tasks include: (1) finishing narrative sections on both applications (particularly the personal statement for Harbor House and the detailed housing history section for Maple Grove), (2) scanning and uploading or attaching all supporting documents (ID, Social Security card, pay stubs, enrollment verification, and therapist letter once available), and (3) confirming with the coach that the applications are complete and ready to be sent. Jordan has expressed that they want to take time to answer the narrative questions thoughtfully, as they see this as an opportunity to explain their goals around stability, education, and mental health. 

Jordan and the coach have agreed that the goal will not be considered complete until at least one full application has been submitted and a confirmation email or letter has been received from the housing program. They expect this to occur later in October 2025, depending on how quickly the last documents are obtained and uploaded.
